heading: Commissioned officers: retention of after completing 18 or more, but less than 20, years of service
label: 12646 Commissioned officers: retention of after completing 18 or more, but less than 20, years of service
notes: Historical and Revision Notes1958ActRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)1006(a)1006(b)1006(c)1006(d)50:1195(a) (less last 30 words).50:1195(b) (less last 30 words).50:1227(c).50:1333(c).50:1181(1) (as applicable to 50:1195)., §§ 102(1) (as applicable to § 205), 205, 307(c), 503(c), , 1151, 1155, 1173; , .Sept. 3, 1954, ch. 125768 Stat. 1149June 30, 1955, ch. 247, § 1(b)69 Stat. 21850:1195(a) (last 30 words).50:1195(b) (last 30 words).1006(e)50:1195(c).In subsections (a) and (b), the words “Notwithstanding any other provisions of this chapter, except as provided in sections 1265 and 1279 of this title” and “has been credited with, or” are omitted as surplusage. The words “entitled to be” in clause (1) are inserted for clarity.In subsection (e), the words “at the end of that period” are substituted for the word “then” for clarity. The words “before the end of that period” are substituted for the word “earlier” for clarity.

After renumbering, such sections now comprise chapter 37 (designated as chapter “1” in chapter analysis) of title 14.Pub. Amendments2018—Subsecs. (a), (b). substituted “under chapter 843,” for “under chapter 573,” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 115–232Subsec. (e)(1). substituted “under section 8373,” for “under section 6389,” and “section 7311, 8323, or 9311” for “section 3911, 6323, or 8911”.Pub. L. 115–232Subsec. (e)(2)(A). substituted “section 7311, 8323, or 9311” for “section 3911, 6323, or 8911”.Pub. L. 115–2321994—, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 103–337, § 1662(h)(2)section 1006 of this titleSubsecs. (a), (b). , substituted “573, 1407, or 1409” for “337, 361, 363, 573, 837, 861, or 863”.Pub. L. 103–337, § 1675(d)(3)(B), substituted “12732” for “1332” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 103–337, § 1675(d)(3)(A)Subsec. (e). , added subsec. (e) and struck out former subsec. (e) which read as follows: “A reserve commissioned officer on active duty (other than for training) who, on the date on which he would otherwise be removed from an active status under section 3846, 3848, 3851, 3852, 6389, 6397, 6403, 6410, 8846, 8848, 8851, or 8852 of this title or , and who is within two years of qualifying for retirement under section 3911, 6323, or 8911 of this title, may, in the discretion of the Secretary concerned, be retained on active duty for a period of not more than two years, if at the end of that period he will be qualified for retirement under one of those sections and will not, before the end of that period, reach the age at which transfer from an active status or discharge is required by this title or title 14. An officer who is retained on active duty under this section may not be removed from an active status while he is on that duty. For officers covered by section 3846, 3848, 3851, or 3852 of this title, the ages at which transfer from an active status or discharge is required are those set forth in section 3843, 3844, or 3845 of this title, or , as the case may be.”Pub. L. 96–513Public Law 85–861substituted “” for “”.Pub. L. 96–322section 740 of title 14section 787 of title 141975—Subsecs. (a), (b). , inserted reference to chapter 21 of title 14.Pub. L. 93–586, § 3(1)Subsec. (c). , substituted “An officer who is retained” for “An officer of the Army or the Air Force who is retained”.Pub. L. 93–586, § 3(2)Subsec. (e). , substituted “discharge is required by this title or title 14” for “discharge is required by this title”.Pub. L. 93–586, § 3(3)1967—Subsec. (e). struck out references to sections 3847 and 8847 of this title.Pub. L. 90–1301962—Subsec. (e). Pub. L. 87—651 substituted “” for “”.section 787 of title 14section 1391 of title 501960—Subsec. (e). struck out references to sections 3849 and 8849 of this title.Pub. L. 86–559

children.text: the date on which he is entitled to be credited with 20 years of service computed under ; orsection 12732 of this title
the third anniversary of the date on which he would otherwise be discharged or transferred from an active status.
the date on which he is entitled to be credited with 20 years of service computed under ; orsection 12732 of this title
the second anniversary of the date on which he would otherwise be discharged or transferred from an active status.

children.label: c
children.text: An officer who is retained in an active status under subsection (a) or (b) is an additional number to those otherwise authorized.

officers who are discharged or transferred from an active status for physical disability, for cause, or because they have reached the age at which transfer from an active status or discharge is required by law; or

A reserve commissioned officer on active duty (other than for training) or full-time National Guard duty (other than full-time National Guard duty for training only) who, on the date on which the officer would otherwise be removed from an active status under section 8373, 14513, or 14514 of this title or section 740 of title 14, is within two years of qualifying for retirement under section 7311, 8323, or 9311 of this title may, in the discretion of the Secretary concerned and subject to paragraph (2), be retained on that duty for a period of not more than two years.1

children.label: A
children.text: at the end of the period for which the officer is retained the officer will be qualified for retirement under section 7311, 8323, or 9311 of this title; and

children.label: B
children.text: the officer will not, before the end of that period, reach the age at which transfer from an active status or discharge is required by this title or title 14.
An officer who is retained on active duty or full-time National Guard duty under this section may not be removed from an active status while on that duty.
